body_txt: A Draconic Humanoid is a fantastic species that can pretty much be described as the offspring of Lizard Folk and Our Dragons Are Different . Not content with simple humanoid reptiles, the writer decided to give them wings , a Breath Weapon , and/or other draconic features. Another option is to put said dragon features onto an otherwise human-like body . These may be the descendants of dragons and normal humanoids , or a species of their own. When these beings are depicted as races, species, or otherwise groups in their own right, they may be given a variety of names. Western media tends to use a variety of modifications of "dragon" — "draconians" is relatively common, with "dragonkin" a little less widespread. "Dragonborn", despite its ubiquity in Dungeons & Dragons , is relatively rare outside of it. Japanese media tends to use "dragonewts" or "draconewts", a term originating from RuneQuest . Note that this does not apply to actual dragons that can shapeshift into human forms, unless they shapeshift into a form which is half human and half dragon. See also Human Aliens when they budget-savingly stay in human form when the camera's running. If suited up in heavy armor, they might be a Dragon Knight . Sub-Trope of Our Dragons Are Different and Monstrous Humanoid . Compare Weredragon , a creature that can change between human and dragon forms. Also related to Demihuman . For kobolds, see Our Kobolds Are Different . For more generic reptile-mammal hybrids, see Reptomammal .
